title: drymocallis glandulosa var. reflexa
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa var. reflexa, also known as the sticky cinquefoil, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The sticky cinquefoil can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
